parts.name: Forehead
parts.text: The patch just above the upper beak along the midline, stark black on the Tufted Titmouse.

parts.name: Lore
parts.text: A small patch between the eye and the upper beak; eyestripes run across the lores and continue behind the eye.

parts.name: Auriculars
parts.text: The feathers just below and behind the eye that cover the ear.

parts.name: Supercilium
parts.text: A stripe of feathers running from the upper beak and up over the eye, much like an eyebrow.

parts.name: Malar Region
parts.text: A patch running from the beak back to the area between the throat and the eye.

parts.name: Throat
parts.text: An area just below the beak on the font of the bird, often brightly colored in male hummingbirds.

parts.name: Side
parts.text: An area adjacent to the wing toward the belly.

parts.name: Flank
parts.text: A broad area on the side of the bird between the wing and leg.

parts.name: Chest
parts.text: A broad area on the front between the wings; on American Robins a rusty red, also called the breast.

parts.name: Belly
parts.text: The patch along the midline above the legs, often covered in feathers from adjacent regions.

parts.name: Undertail Coverts
parts.text: Feathers between the legs and under the tail that smooth the airflow over the base of the tail feathers.

parts.name: Tail
parts.text: A set of stiff feathers at the rear of the bird arranged in symmetrical pairs on either side of the midline; most birds have six pairs.

parts.name: Uppertail Coverts
parts.text: Feathers just above the tail that smooth airflow over the base of the tail feathers.

parts.name: Rump
parts.text: An area on the back of the bird below the mantle and above the tail.

parts.name: Underwing Coverts
parts.text: Feathers on the underside of the wing that smooth the airflow over the base of the wing feathers.

parts.name: Primaries
parts.text: Stiff feathers on the outer portion (hand) of the wing that generate forward thrust during flight.

parts.name: Secondaries
parts.text: Stiff feathers on the inner-middle portion (forearm) of the wing that generate lift during flight.

parts.name: Crown
parts.text: The area on the very top of the head along the midline.

parts.name: Mantle
parts.text: The area between the wings on a bird’s back.

parts.name: Nape
parts.text: The area on the back of a bird’s head and neck.

parts.name: Outer Ear
parts.text: A small indentation in the skin leading to the ear canal.

parts.name: Apteria
parts.text: Regions of bare or sparsely feathered skin.

parts.name: Pterylae
parts.text: Clusters of feather follicles that produce groups of feathers.

parts.name: Oil Gland
parts.text: A gland at the base of the tail; secretes preening oils.

parts.name: Flight Feather Shafts
parts.text: The strong central shafts of the flight feathers.

parts.name: Podotheca
parts.text: The skin covering a bird’s foot.

parts.name: Beak
parts.text: A bony structure extending above and below a bird’s mouth.

parts.name: Tongue
parts.text: Helps a bird manipulate food.

parts.name: Stiffness Neck
parts.text: Muscles along the neck.

parts.name: Pectoralis
parts.text: Powerful downstroke muscle.

parts.name: Humerus
parts.text: The thickest bone of the wing.

parts.name: Sternum
parts.text: The breastbone with a keel.

parts.name: Heart
parts.text: Propels blood into arteries.

parts.name: Lung
parts.text: Exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide.
